title: Garlicky Dense Malaysian Shiitake Mushrooms Stir-fry
description: A Mediterranean-friendly meal-prep dish featuring shiitake mushrooms simmered with garlic and other aromatic spices to create a dense, flavorful sauce that pairs well with rice or naan bread.
instructions: Wash the shiitake mushrooms and chop them into small pieces.
Finely mince the garlic cloves, ginger, and roughly chop the tomato paste.
Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic, crushed red pepper flakes (optional), and sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chopped shiitake mushrooms to the pan and toss with the garlic mixture for approximately 3 minutes until they start to release moisture.
Pour in tomato paste and stir well for an additional 2-3 minutes allowing it to thicken slightly into a sauce.
Stir in soy sauce, fish sauce, sesame oil, salt, and pepper. Adjust seasoning to taste with more salt or pepper as needed.
Let the mixture simmer on low heat for about 10 minutes until most of the liquid has evaporated and the sauce is dense and flavorful.
Once done, transfer the shiitake mushroom sauce into a container for meal prep or serving directly from the pan. It's best to serve with rice or naan bread.
